highlights.text: Heading into CPI on Tuesday, I was looking for this rally to continue and I stated at Monday’s close that: “Its been a hugely profitable run and I am +250 points now on my most recent long..My general lean is always to defer to the trend until it ends.
I concluded yesterday by writing: “My general lean is to defer to it.
They put in my core setup, the Failed Breakdown, whereby they flush a major low, trap shorts, recover the low, then squeeze.

Last Wednesday after FOMC, we saw a massive Failed Breakdown where we flushed the prior days low at 5606, recovered, and I was long since then for what turned into a 300+ point rally, one of the largest of the year.
This means bulls want to hold 5850, 5815 on any CPI flushes (I’ll be watching for failed breakdowns).
For yesterday, it was the same drill: Bulls would want to to put in Failed Breakdowns, defend the flag, then break it out.
We dipped under the flag, recovered it by yesterday to put in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, and ran to 5945 1st target which was the high of day yesterday.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss a variant of my core setup (the Failed Breakdown) I call the "double/triple dip” Failed Breakdown which refers to when price puts in multiple Failed Breakdowns at the same level , representing high-level institutional accumulation.
Next target slate is 5890, 5910, 5926.” By Tuesday, we tagged 5926 exact, and spent the rest of the day Tuesday and yesterday building out another bull flag, which had resistance at 5925.

post_title: Will Next Week Bring A Pullback For SPX? May 19th Plan
